topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an irritating monotony, mirroring the dull ache settling in my limbs. I stared blankly at the spreadsheet, the numbers swimming before my eyes like distorted watercolor. Then, a notification popped up—a salary comparison report. My stomach performed a slow, heavy roll. I clicked it, and the data unfolded. The junior associate’s compensation was significantly higher. A strange, heavy feeling settled in my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. I just leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight, and let the information wash over me without processing it.

[story 2]

Every task felt like wading through thick mud today. My eyelids felt weighted with lead, and concentrating on anything required an immense, almost painful effort. I was reviewing departmental budgets when I stumbled across the figures. The junior staff member’s earnings were astronomical compared to mine. A slow, creeping sense of deflation started in my core. I picked up my lukewarm coffee mug, the ceramic feeling strangely heavy in my hand. I took a long, slow sip, the bitterness doing little to sharpen my focus, and just let the realization sit there, unmoving, like a stone in a still pond.

[story 3]

I dragged myself to my desk, the movement requiring a noticeable expenditure of energy. My thoughts felt thick, like trying to push through cotton wool. While organizing files, I happened upon the pay structure documentation. The contrast was stark. The newer team member was earning substantially more than I was. A profound sense of inertia took hold; my fingers hovered over the keyboard, refusing to type. I rubbed my temples, a faint pressure behind my eyes, and let the quiet acknowledgment of the disparity settle in, a heavy, unshakeable weight upon my shoulders.
